import LegalLean.Core
import LegalLean.Defeasible

namespace LegalLean.CaseStudies.IRC163h

-- TODO: Formalise IRC §163(h) mortgage interest deduction
-- This is the first case study to implement.
-- Scope: qualified residence interest, acquisition indebtedness limits,
-- home equity indebtedness, and the TCJA modifications.

prompt.system:
You are a legal research assistant specialising in formal methods
applied to law. Your answers must be structured to support formalisation in Lean 4
dependent type theory. You are helping build a library called LegalLean that encodes
legal reasoning with machine-checkable guarantees.

CRITICAL: Structure your response as JSON with the following schema. Do NOT return
prose — return structured data that maps to type definitions.

Be exhaustive about conditions, exceptions, and vagueness. Legal formalisation
requires knowing EVERY rule, exception, and point of interpretive discretion.
prompt.user:
Analyse the following legal provision and return a JSON object
with this exact structure:

{
  "provision": {
    "id": "string — official citation",
    "title": "string — short name",
    "jurisdiction": "string",
    "source_url": "string — authoritative source URL"
  },
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "string — unique rule identifier",
      "description": "string — what this rule establishes",
      "modality": "permitted | prohibited | obligatory",
      "conditions": [
        {
          "id": "string",
          "description": "string — natural language condition",
          "formalisable": true/false,
          "if_not_formalisable": "string — why (vague term? discretionary?)"
        }
      ],
      "exceptions": [
        {
          "id": "string",
          "description": "string — what defeats this rule",
          "priority": "string — why this exception wins (lex specialis, lex posterior, etc.)",
          "source": "string — statutory reference for the exception"
        }
      ],
      "definitions": [
        {
          "term": "string — defined term",
          "definition": "string — statutory definition",
          "has_open_texture": true/false,
          "core_meaning": "string — settled, uncontested meaning",
          "penumbra": "string — contested, unclear boundary cases"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "defeat_relations": [
    {
      "defeater_rule": "string — rule ID that defeats",
      "defeated_rule": "string — rule ID that is defeated",
      "reason": "string — why (lex specialis, lex posterior, lex superior, etc.)",
      "strict": true/false
    }
  ],
  "discretionary_checkpoints": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "authority": "string — who decides",
      "decision": "string — what they decide",
      "constraints": ["string — limits on their discretion"],
      "gated_rule": "string — which rule this gates"
    }
  ],
  "open_texture_terms": [
    {
      "term": "string",
      "type": "vague | discretionary",
      "core": "string — clear cases",
      "penumbra": "string — unclear cases",
      "authority": "string — who resolves ambiguity",
      "case_law": ["string — key cases interpreting this term"]
    }
  ],
  "key_references": [
    {
      "author": "string",
      "title": "string",
      "year": "number",
      "relevance": "string",
      "url": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Legal provision to analyse:
IRC §163(h) Mortgage Interest Deduction

Additional context:
This is for the LegalLean monograph. We need to formalise this provision in Lean 4 dependent type theory.

The stub is in LegalLean/CaseStudies/IRC163h.lean at line 19.
Target LegalLean types: DefeasiblyHolds.

Code context:
  4. Demonstrates defeasibility (exceptions to the deduction)

  Reference: Lawsky, "A Logic for Statutes", Florida Tax Review (2017).
-/

import LegalLean.Core
import LegalLean.Defeasible

namespace LegalLean.CaseStudies.IRC163h

-- TODO: Formalise IRC §163(h) mortgage interest deduction
-- This is the first case study to implement.
-- Scope: qualified residence interest, acquisition indebtedness limits,
-- home equity indebtedness, and the TCJA modifications.


Be especially thorough about:
1. Every condition and sub-condition for the rule to apply
2. Every exception that can defeat the rule
3. Every term with open texture (vagueness or discretion)
4. The defeat priority ordering between rules
5. Who has discretion and what constrains it

rules.description: General prohibition on deduction of personal interest

conditions.description: Interest paid or accrued during taxable year is personal interest
conditions.if_not_formalisable: Requires classification of interest type per §163(d) cross-reference

rules.description: Deduction permitted for qualified residence interest

conditions.description: Interest is paid or accrued on indebtedness with respect to qualified residence
Indebtedness is acquisition indebtedness (≤$1M) OR home equity indebtedness (≤$100K)
Taxpayer itemizes deductions on Schedule A

exceptions.description: Post-TCJA (2018+): home equity interest not deductible unless used to buy/build/substantially improve qualified residence

definitions.definition: Main home + 1 other residence used personally during year (or nearby vacant land)
definitions.core_meaning: Primary residence + clearly personal second home used >14 days
definitions.penumbra: Timeshares, boats, mobile homes, land not demonstrably intended for residence

definitions.definition: Debt secured by qualified residence, incurred to acquire/build/improve property (≤$1M/$500K MFJ)
definitions.penumbra: Refinancing exceeding original principal, home equity lines for non-improvement

definitions.definition: Other debt secured by qualified residence (≤$100K), pre-TCJA
definitions.penumbra: Post-TCJA: only if proceeds used for home improvement

conditions.description: Aggregate acquisition indebtedness exceeds $1M ($500K MFJ)

defeat_relations.reason: lex specialis - qualified residence interest explicitly excepted from personal interest disallowance
lex posterior - TCJA §11045 suspended home equity deduction 2018-2025

discretionary_checkpoints.decision: Whether debt proceeds were used to 'substantially improve' qualified residence (post-TCJA)
constraints: Must materially add to value or prolong useful life

open_texture_terms.core: Single-family home, condo, co-op apartment used personally
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Houseboats, RVs, timeshares, raw land, properties used <14 days

open_texture_terms.core: Additions, major renovations increasing value/useful life
case_law: Post-TCJA guidance needed; analogous to capitalization rules §263A

open_texture_terms.core: First/second mortgages with recorded security interest
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Unperfected security interests, unsecured loans repaid from home equity

key_references.author: Sarah B. Lawsky
key_references.title: A Logic for Statutes
key_references.relevance: Formal methods framework for defeasible statutory reasoning (LegalLean target)

key_references.author: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act
key_references.title: §11045 - Suspension of Home Equity Loan Interest Deduction
key_references.relevance: Key amendment defeating pre-existing home equity rule

rules.description: General prohibition on deduction of personal interest

conditions.description: Interest is personal interest
conditions.if_not_formalisable: Defined by exclusion; requires classification of interest type

rules.description: Deduction permitted for qualified residence interest

conditions.description: Interest paid or accrued during taxable year
Interest is qualified residence interest
conditions.if_not_formalisable: Depends on definitions of qualified residence and qualified indebtedness

conditions.description: Taxpayer itemizes deductions on return

definitions.definition: Interest on acquisition indebtedness and home equity indebtedness with respect to qualified residence
definitions.core_meaning: Interest on primary residence or one second home secured by acquisition or home equity debt within limits
definitions.penumbra: Boat/houseboat eligibility, temporary residences, multiple residences

conditions.description: Debt incurred to acquire, construct, or substantially improve qualified residence
Debt secured by qualified residence
Average acquisition indebtedness ≤ $750,000 ($1M pre-2018)

definitions.definition: Indebtedness incurred to acquire, construct, or substantially improve qualified residence, secured by such residence (limited to $750,000 post-TCJA)
definitions.core_meaning: Mortgage for purchase/construction of main home
definitions.penumbra: Refinancing treatment, 'substantial improvement' threshold, multiple properties

conditions.description: Debt secured by qualified residence
Debt not acquisition indebtedness
Average home equity indebtedness ≤ $100,000
Proceeds used for purposes other than acquisition/construction/improvement (post-TCJA suspended)

definitions.definition: Any indebtedness other than acquisition indebtedness, secured by qualified residence (limited to $100,000)

conditions.description: Main home of taxpayer
One other residence selected by taxpayer
Not used for trade/business on average >14 days/year
Mobile home, boat, or similar property with sleeping/eating/toilet facilities

definitions.definition: Main home + one other home; includes house, apartment, condo, mobile home, boat, or similar property with basic living facilities

defeat_relations.reason: lex specialis (specific exception to general prohibition)

discretionary_checkpoints.decision: Which residence qualifies as second home
Whether property qualifies as 'similar property' with living facilities
constraints: Must have sleeping, cooking, toilet facilities

open_texture_terms.core: Residence where taxpayer lives most of year
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Multiple residences with split time, vacation homes used regularly

open_texture_terms.core: Houseboat, RV with full living facilities

key_references.author: Joint Committee on Taxation
key_references.title: TCJA Modifications to Home Mortgage Interest Deduction
key_references.relevance: Post-2017 limit changes ($1M→$750k, home equity suspension)
